original_reasoning: نسطاس=physician/knower (from Greek); γνωστός=known. Both from gnosis root, weak semantic link

Arabic فول is a plant term and Greek target is also a plant designation; direct botanical category overlap is plausible.

الشناعة: شنيع (ugly, repulsive, abominable). λυσαλγής=in painful distress. What is شنيع (repulsive/ugly) causes suffering in the beholder, connecting to λυσαλγής (painful distress). Both describe intense negative experiences, one aesthetic and one physical.
Arabic luminous moonlight can meet the pale-color side of grey at a conceptual level.
Arabic فان/بهاء carries 'splendor, appearance'; Greek ἐπιφάνεια = 'appearance, manifestation (esp. divine)'. Plausible conceptual echo of showing/radiance.
